ORDER RE: DEMURRER TO MIKE JOZINOVIC AND MARIJA JOZINOVIC DBA MJ TILE'S FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT BY PJ CON, INC., AND JOHN R. NEVAREZ

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS PJ CON, INC. AND JOHN R. NEVAREZ'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO MIKE JOZINOVIC'S CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. THE ALLEGATION THAT MIKE WAS A CONTRACTING PARTY IS A LEGAL CONCLUSION AND THE MORE SPECIFIC ALLEGATIONS INDICATE THAT ONLY MJ TILE, MARIJA JOZINOVIC'S SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP, WAS THE CONTRACTING PARTY. (SEE PARS. BC-1, 3, AND EX. A OF THE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO MIKE JOZINOVIC'S CAUSE OF ACTION FOR COMMON COUNTS. (SEE HARRIS V. KESSLER (1932) 124 CAL.APP. 299, 303.) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO MIKE JOZINOVIC'S CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD. THE REPRESENTATIONS WERE BEING MADE TO MJ TILE. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO MJ TILE'S CLAIM FOR FRAUD. PARS. FR-1-6 ALLEGE THE CLAIM WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY. THE MISREPRESENTATION OCCURRED ON 6/20/16, JOHN NEVAREZ MADE THE MISREPRESENTATION BY ELECTRONIC MESSAGE, MJ TILE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES SCIENTER AND RELIANCE, AND LESS PARTICULARITY IS REQUIRED WHERE THE FACTS ARE PRESUMPTIVELY WITHIN CROSS-DEFENDANTS' KNOWLEDGE. (SEE TURNER V. MILSTEIN (1951) 103 CAL.APP.2D 651, 658.) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Cross-defendants PJ Con, Inc. and John R. Nevarez's demurrer to amended cross complaint is:

SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to Mike Jozinovic's cause of action for breach of contract. The allegation that Mike was a contracting party is a legal conclusion and the more specific allegations indicate that only MJ Tile, Marija Jozinovic's sole proprietorship, was the contracting party. (See pars. BC-1, 3, and Ex. A of the Amended Cross-Complaint.)

SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to Mike Jozinovic's cause of action for common counts. (See Harris v. Kessler (1932) 124 Cal.App. 299, 303.)

SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to Mike Jozinovic's cause of action for fraud. The representations were being made to MJ Tile. The demurrer is OVERRULED as to MJ Tile's claim for fraud. Pars. FR-1-6 allege the claim with sufficient particularity. The misrepresentation occurred on 6/20/16, John Nevarez made the misrepresentation by electronic message, MJ Tile sufficiently alleges scienter and reliance, and less particularity is required where the facts are presumptively within cross-defendants' knowledge. (See Turner v. Milstein (1951) 103 Cal.App.2d 651, 658.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kevin P. Lee, Esq. T:951.532.7215, for defendants/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTs JOHN NERAREZ and PJ CON, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT as follows:

Defendants PJ Con, Inc. and John Nevarez's demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by Mike and Marija Jozinovic is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to all three causes of action. As to the breach of contract and common counts causes of action, the cross-complaint does not adequately allege the nature of the "services" or the "substance" of the contract's terms. In addition, there is insufficient basis alleged for Mr. Jozinovic basis for asserting a contract or common counts claim. At a minimum, Mr. Jazinovic needs to allege how he individually and the sole proprietorship entered into an agreement with PJ Con. The fraud cause of action should be alleged with more particularity. The Jozinovics should clarify that the representation was oral and what other projects they continued to work on because of the alleged falsehood.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Language From First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS PJ CON, INC., JOHN R. NEVAREZ and PATRICIA LOREE-NEVAREZ'S Motion To Strike Language From First Amended Complaint is DENIED. Defendants' position that the language they seek to strike is contrary to the parties' contract is not amenable to resolution on a motion to strike. Because the contract is reasonably susceptible to the language sought to be stricken, extrinsic evidence is necessary to determine if plaintiff's interpretation of the contract is correct. (George v. Automobile Club of Southern California (2011) 201 Cal. App. 4th 1112). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Off Calendar Apr-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PJ CON, INC., PATRICIA LOREE-NEVAREZ and JOHN NEVAREZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled as to the first, fourth and sixth claims and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all other claims. Defendants have withdrawn their demurrer as to the first, fourth and sixth claims. The other four claims are insufficiently pled as to any defendant. The implied covenant claim fails to allege any breach apart from the breach of an express term. The implied warranty claim fails to identify any products that were not as impliedly warranted. The intentional misrepresentation and false promise claims fail to allege which defendant made the assertedly actionable statements, when were they made, and to whom. Order signed.
